search_queries_used: site:kdigo.org 2024 CKD monitoring GFR albuminuria cardiovascular risk
site:nice.org.uk NG238 chronic kidney disease cardiovascular risk
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic CKD staging or cardiovascular-risk calculation
automatic kidney or cardiovascular medicine, dose, or referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: CKD status and progression require verified chronicity, GFR, albuminuria, and clinical context.
No score, medicine, dose, treatment, or referral was generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults with clinician-established or suspected CKD undergoing cardiovascular-risk documentation.
setting_applicability: Primary, kidney, and cardiovascular outpatient review; acute kidney injury and unstable cardiovascular presentations require direct care.
UAE_applicability: International KDIGO and NICE evidence requires UAE laboratory, risk-tool, kidney, cardiology, prescribing, referral, and facility adaptation.
recency_verification: Current KDIGO 2024 and NICE NG238 sources were reviewed on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: KDIGO 2024 and NICE NG238 remain current.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The KDIGO section supports clinician review of kidney function and albuminuria over time and documented change or progression context without automatically classifying CKD or cardiovascular risk.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-arranged GFR and albuminuria monitoring and evaluation of a greater-than-20-percent eGFR change or ACR doubling without mapping fixed legacy intervals.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The NICE section supports clinician-led cardiovascular-risk review that includes kidney disease and patient preferences without generating a risk score or treatment.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-led cardiovascular risk review and documented lifestyle discussion without autonomously generating advice.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The KDIGO stewardship section was used only to support documenting clinician review of medicine-related kidney context; no medicine, dose, hold, or substitution is generated.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports periodic medication review, accurate medication lists, adherence, continued indication, interaction, monitoring, reconciliation, and nephrotoxic-medicine context without treatment changes or doses.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Kidney referral criteria were reviewed for clinician context, but no referral is generated from the generic cardiac-risk workflow.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-led referral documentation for qualified sustained GFR decline, significant albuminuria with haematuria, refractory hypertension, persistent potassium abnormalities, and other listed circumstances.

unresolved_source_gaps: Kidney diagnosis and cause, chronicity, eGFR and albuminuria values and trends, blood pressure, diabetes, lipids, smoking, family history, medicines, adherence, symptoms, examination, validated cardiovascular risk, treatment, referral, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
